description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that uses a dynamically built Docker image `${DEFAULT_BRANCH_IMAGE}` (derived from `${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}/mise-bootstrapped-verify:${CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG}`) as the default image for all jobs, with tags `gitlab-org` and a timeout of 3h. Define global variables for Ruby bundler, Go cache, Node cache, Hugo version, and many GDK internal paths, and set a shared before_script that raises the inotify watch limit and sources `support/ci/utils.sh` for debugging, plus an after_script that moves GitLab and GDK logs into `gitlab_log` and `gdk_log` directories. Include external CI templates that provide jobs across stages `build`, `test`, `analyze`, `pre-verify`, `integration`, `verify`, `novel`, `deploy`, and `cleanup`, each job inheriting the default image and variables and using the cached bundle path `${GDK_INTERNAL_CACHE_RUBY_FULL_DIR}/bundle` and Go cache `${GDK_INTERNAL_CACHE_GO_FULL_DIR}/build`. Ensure that jobs use the defined caches (e.g., `vendor/bundle`, Go build cache) and that any job that runs the GDK verification steps uses the `VERIFY_IMAGE` variable to pull `${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}/mise-bootstrapped-gdk-installed:${CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG}` and runs the verification script, while integration jobs use `INTEGRATION_IMAGE` similarly. Apply rules so that merge‑request pipelines are triggered via the included `Workflows/MergeRequest-Pipelines.gitlab-ci.yml`, and the pipeline is interruptible as defined in the `_interruptible.yml` include, with no explicit artifacts defined here but inherited from the included templates.
